prevalence.trend: Increasing due to aging population and rising NCDs

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study (India-specific estimates)

vulnerablePopulations: Rural populations with limited access to care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ECG showing irregular RR intervals with no discernible P waves; confirmed by Holter monitoring if needed (ESC guidelines adapted for India)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Standard ECG-based diagnosis; pulse palpation in resource-limited settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Rate control with beta-blockers; anticoagulation with warfarin or DOACs; rhythm control in select cases (per Indian Heart Association guidelines)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Low awareness, rural-urban disparity in access, high cost of anticoagulants, rheumatic heart disease prevalence
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Expanding cardiology networks in urban centers, government NCD programs like NPCDCS
